info.title: Retrieve Warranty Confirmations API
info.description:
Retrieves a **list of PINs** (product identification numbers) that were **registered within a specified date range** for a dealer.

The legacy SOAP **Retrieve Warranty Confirms** operation maps to this REST API (**Retrieve Warranty Confirmation API**). Use your **SOAP-to-REST field mapping** and **REST API FAQ** attachments for property-level details.

Support: Warranty_Api_Support@JohnDeere.com

servers.description: Sandbox (cert). Production uses `https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com` — confirm the path prefix under `/digcust/wy/` and whether the `cert` segment is present for your environment.

OAuth2.description: John Deere Dealer Solutions OAuth 2.0. Use the **authorization code** flow (three-legged) for interactive users, or the **client credentials** grant for machine-to-machine access. Request the OAuth scope assigned to your application for this API (this reference uses `warranty` as an example — confirm the scope in your Developer.Deere.com application and technical documentation).

WtyHeaderEntity.description: OData entity from **WtyHeaderSet** / related **WtyConf** data. Exact properties follow your OData contract and SOAP-to-REST mapping; additional fields may appear.

pin.description: Product identification number when returned for a confirmation row.

dealer.description: Dealer identifier aligned with the **Dealer** key in the request.

beginDate.description: Begin of registration window (format per service contract, often ISO date).

endDate.description: End of registration window (format per service contract, often ISO date).

WtyHeaderSetCollection.description: OData collection returned from **WtyHeaderSet** (may include `@odata.context` and other OData annotations).

value.description: Warranty confirmation header rows matching the dealer and date range.

ODataBatchResponse.description: OData **$batch** response as **multipart/mixed** MIME parts (per OData batch protocol).

message.example: The WtyHeaderSet key or date range could not be parsed.

get.description:
Retrieves **WtyHeaderSet** entities for the given **Dealer** and **BeginDate** / **EndDate** range — the REST successor to SOAP **Retrieve Warranty Confirms**.

**Sandbox example:** `GET https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/DbsWtyConfirmations/WtyHeaderSet(Dealer='550012345',BeginDate='2026-01-01',EndDate='2026-03-31')`

**Production:** Use the production host (`https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com`) with the path pattern documented for your program (the `cert` segment or application id may differ from sandbox).

**Accept:** `application/json`

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Application or service instance segment for your Dealer Solutions integration (sandbox example: `DbsWtyConfirmations`).

parameters.name: Dealer
parameters.description: Dealer identifier in the WtyHeaderSet key.

parameters.name: BeginDate
parameters.description: Start date for the registration range (format per OData contract; use the same format as in John Deere examples).

parameters.name: EndDate
parameters.description: End date for the registration range (format per OData contract).

200.description: Warranty confirmation headers (PINs registered in the date range, per service contract).

400.description: Bad request — malformed key or invalid date parameters.

error.message: The WtyHeaderSet key or date range could not be parsed.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid access token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not include the required scope or organization access.

error.message: Access token is missing the required scope.

404.description: No data found for the supplied dealer and date range (when the service returns 404 for empty results).

error.message: No warranty confirmations were found for the supplied criteria.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

post.description:
Groups multiple OData requests into a **single** HTTP request using the **$batch** endpoint. See [OData batch requests](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_BatchRequests). Request and response bodies use **multipart/mixed** per OData batch semantics.

**Sandbox example:** `POST https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/DbsWtyConfirmations/$batch`

**Accept / Content-Type:** `multipart/mixed` (include a valid MIME **boundary** in the Content-Type header for the request body).

**Production:** Use the production host (`https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com`) with the path pattern documented for your program.

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Application or service instance segment for your Dealer Solutions integration (sandbox example: `DbsWtyConfirmations`).

requestBody.description: OData batch request body as **multipart/mixed** (batch boundary and MIME parts per OData specification).

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request POST \
  --url "https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/DbsWtyConfirmations/$batch" \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' \
  --header 'Accept: multipart/mixed' \
  --header 'Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=batch_12345' \
  --data-binary @batch-request.mime

200.description: Batch completed; response is **multipart/mixed** containing per-request results.

202.description: Batch accepted (when the service returns 202 for async batch processing).

400.description: Bad request — malformed batch payload or boundary.

error.message: The batch request could not be parsed.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid access token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not include the required scope or organization access.

error.message: Access token is missing the required scope.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.
